scenario: Persisted Redux store with rehydration timing.
alex_initial_position: Block app render until rehydration complete.
sam_initial_position: Render with empty state, hydrate async.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Render with empty state shows wrong UI briefly.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For IDB (async): show skeleton during hydration.

decision_framework: Block with skeleton for state-dependent UI.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors render before hydration. Senior insight: stale state showing then flipping is bad UX.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hooks compose better. HOC wrapper hell.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Class components are legacy. New code: hooks.

context_dependent_answer: Hooks. HOCs only for class components.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors copy HOC patterns. Senior insight: hooks superseded HOCs.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: After first error, fast feedback via onChange.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hybrid - onBlur first, then onChange. Standard.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors validate onSubmit only. Senior insight: progressive feedback during interaction.

scenario: Pagination total count: fetch separately vs in-response.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Count in same query is one trip.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: COUNT(*) over millions of rows is slow.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Estimated count for huge tables. Exact for small.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Estimated count or skip total.

context_dependent_answer: Small: exact. Large: estimate. Cursor: no count.
decision_framework: Cursor for large. Offset for small. Count expensive at scale.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors require count. Senior insight: count is a perf tax. Skip if not needed.

scenario: CSS Grid vs Flexbox for layout.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For dashboards (grid of widgets), Grid wins.

code_examples.code:
// Page layout - Grid
...
Sidebar
Content
// Component layout - Flex
Label

what_juniors_miss: Juniors flex everything. Senior insight: Grid for 2D layouts is much cleaner.

alex_initial_position: Same-document for SPA. Cross-document for MPA.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Cross-document (MPA) View Transitions are newer but exist.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For Astro/Next App router with hard navigations, cross-document.

context_dependent_answer: SPA: same-document. MPA: cross-document where supported.
decision_framework: SPA: same. MPA: cross when available.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't know cross-document exists. Senior insight: view transitions span document types.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Factories for variation. Fixtures for representative.

context_dependent_answer: Factories for variants. Fixtures for known data.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors only factories. Senior insight: known fixtures useful for reproducibility.

scenario: Public API: type aliases vs interfaces.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Type can express unions, intersections, conditional.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Interface allows consumers to extend via merging.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For shape: either. For complex: type.

context_dependent_answer: Mix: type for complex, interface for object shapes.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use one. Senior insight: type and interface have different strengths.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Preemptive optimization adds noise without measured benefit.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Some patterns (memo on list item) are universally good.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted with Compiler. Without: some preemptive.

context_dependent_answer: With Compiler: profile-driven. Without: targeted preemptive.
decision_framework: Profile, then optimize. Or use React Compiler.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors memo defensively. Senior insight: defensive memo is unmeasured cost.

scenario: Source map types: source-map vs cheap-module-source-map.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Dev: line. Prod: full source-map (uploaded to Sentry).

what_juniors_miss: Juniors same maps for all envs. Senior insight: source maps trade speed vs accuracy.

scenario: Custom hooks distribution: in-package vs separate.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors split packages. Senior insight: sub-paths achieve same with less overhead.

alex_initial_position: Cleanup errors are swallowed by React. Report via try/catch.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't handle cleanup errors. Senior insight: errors in cleanup are real and silent in older React.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Unused CSS still parsed by browser. Cost.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Gzipped CSS is small. Parsing fast.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For large apps, per-route CSS measurable.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors one CSS bundle. Senior insight: CSS can split with code.

scenario: Keyboard navigation: tab order via tabindex vs DOM order.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For visual order != DOM order (grid layouts), needed.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Sometimes restructure isn't possible. tabindex carefully.

context_dependent_answer: DOM order default. tabindex 0/-1 for managed focus only.
decision_framework: DOM order. tabindex=0/-1 for managed focus.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't tabindex. Senior insight: tabindex=-1 + JS keyboard nav is the pattern for custom widgets.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Barrels break tree-shaking and slow IDE.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Type-only barrels OK. Value-export barrels: case by case.

context_dependent_answer: Barrels at module boundaries (features/). Avoid deep barrels.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors barrel everything or nothing. Senior insight: barrels at module boundaries define API.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted for simple cases. XState for complex flows.

context_dependent_answer: Simple: reducer + DU. Complex flow: XState.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors booleans. Senior insight: DU prevents invalid state combinations.

alex_initial_position: Hooks replace containers. Just rendering components.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: With hooks, separation is in hook vs component, not extra wrapper.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Containers test rendering with mock data easily.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hook + presenter (no wrapper): hook handles data, presenter is pure.

context_dependent_answer: Hooks for data + pure presenter. Skip container wrapper.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mix data + render. Senior insight: separation aids testing and clarity.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For RHF forms with arrays, useFieldArray.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors useState arrays in RHF forms. Senior insight: useFieldArray handles stable keys correctly.

context: User on dashboard; prefetch all sibling routes?
context_dependent_answer: Top 2-3 likely-next routes on idle, respect saveData.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors prefetch all. Senior insight: prefetch is a cost. Selective wins.

scenario: Tailwind theme: extend defaults vs replace.
alex_initial_position: Extend. Keep Tailwind defaults + add custom.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Default spacing (1, 2, 4) is well-thought. Useful even with custom.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Custom colors only. Defaults conflict with design.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors keep all defaults. Senior insight: own your tokens. Tailwind defaults are starting point.

code_examples.code:
Bouncing icon (only if motion safe)
Animated content

what_juniors_miss: Juniors animate without check. Senior insight: motion preferences are A11y signals.
Juniors trust global coverage. Senior insight: critical paths need higher bar.
Juniors write Exclude. Senior insight: NonNullable is the standard idiom.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Effects still run on main thread post-render. Idle yields.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For analytics during commit, defer to idle.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors run analytics in commit. Senior insight: analytics shouldn't block.

scenario: Hot Module Replacement (HMR) vs full reload.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: HMR keeps state during edit. Faster iteration.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: HMR sometimes breaks - then state is wrong.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For most edits, HMR works. Vite HMR is fast/reliable.

what_juniors_miss: Juniors disable HMR after one issue. Senior insight: HMR is a productivity multiplier.
